channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Northerners will never vote for Peter Obi – Ex-Borno gov Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Borno Governor Ali Modu Sheriff claims Northerners will never vote for Peter Obi in 2027, citing grievances from his time as Anambra governor.
Read More: https://punchng.com/northerners-will-never-vote-for-peter-obi-ex-borno-gov-sheriff/

item.title: ‎FG seeks Sowore’s remand over unmet bail conditions
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has urged an Abuja court to remand Omoyele Sowore over his alleged failure to meet fresh N200m Sowore bail conditions.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-seeks-sowores-remand-over-unmet-bail-conditions/

item.title: ‎PHOTOS: World’s most premature baby marks second birthday
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nash Keen, the world’s most premature baby, has defied expectations by celebrating his second birthday. Born at 21 weeks, his journey inspires many.
Read More: https://punchng.com/photos-worlds-most-premature-baby-marks-second-birthday/

item.title: Police arrest 150, recover 100 motorcycles, firearms in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Police Command arrests 150 suspects, recovers 100 motorcycles, firearms, and drugs in a major raid on criminal hideouts in Igando and Idimu.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-150-recover-100-motorcycles-firearms-in-lagos/

item.title: ‎Edo security outfit asks businesses to install CCTV by July 30
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Edo State Security Squad mandates businesses to install CCTV cameras by July 30 to enhance security and combat crime. Learn more about the new directive.
Read More: https://punchng.com/edo-security-outfit-asks-businesses-to-install-cctv-by-july-30/

item.title: States now get my eight-year allocation in months — Ex-Borno gov Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ex-Borno Gov. Ali Modu Sheriff credits President Tinubu’s economic reforms, including fuel subsidy removal, for significant increases in state allocations.
Read More: https://punchng.com/states-now-get-eight-years-allocation-in-months-ex-borno-gov-sheriff/

item.title: Mbappe condemns racist remarks made by Paraguayan senator
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Kylian Mbappe has described a Paraguayan politician as "despicable" and unworthy of her position after she made several racist remarks toward the France star.

item.title: Court strikes out ADC official’s suit, awards N100m costs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Federal High Court strikes out ADC official Nkemakolam Ukandu’s suit against judges for lack of diligent prosecution, awarding N100m costs.
Read More: https://punchng.com/court-strikes-out-adc-officials-suit-awards-n100m-costs/

item.title: 'I thought I was done!' - Menzies bounces back with title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Cameron Menzies claimed his first title of the year at Players Championship 23, defeating Cristo Reyes 8-5 in Monday's final.

item.title: Edo Warns Cultists Against 7/7 Celebrations, Arrests 2 Suspects With Firearms
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Edo State Government has warned suspected cult groups against holding the annual July 7 (“7/7”) celebrations across the state, vowing to arrest and prosecute anyone found participating in the event as part of its intensified crackdown on cult-related violence and insecurity. The warn

item.title: Kwara APC Guber Candidate, Danladi- Salihu, Others Receive Emir Of Ilorin’s Blessings
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Emir of Ilorin, Alh Ibrahim Sulu-Gambari on Monday offered his official blessings for the gubernatorial candidate of the All Progressives Congress(APC) in Kwara State,Engr. Yakubu Danladi- Salihu. The Emir also extended his blessings to all the APC’s 34 senatorial, House of Represen

item.title: Burna Boy hits 644m monthly audience on YouTube music
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Burna Boy achieves a massive 644 million monthly audience on YouTube Music, becoming the top Nigerian artist on the platform. Discover his latest streaming
Read More: https://punchng.com/burna-boy-hits-644m-monthly-audience-on-youtube-music/

item.title: Mbappé fires back at Paraguayan senator after racist abuse
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Mbappe's reaction came after Celeste Amarilla attacked the Real Madrid superstar in the wake of Saturday's game in Philadelphia, a bad-tempered affair which France won 1-0. The post Mbappé fires back at Paraguayan senator after racist abuse appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Prince Adeyemi: Ogbomoso Elders Disown Culprit In Fake Agency Scandal
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Say he has a track record of fraud Prominent elders and leaders from Ogbomoso have strongly distanced themselves from Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew, the man at the centre of the ongoing fake presidential agency scandal rocking the nation. In a statement issued after an emergency meeting and

item.title: World Cup 2026: Cape Verde’s Cabral pips Messi to Round of 32 Best Goal honour
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Cape Verde defender Lopes Cabral has been named winner of Goal of Round 32 at the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026, DAILY POST reports. Cabral won the award for his superb strike in Cape Verde’s pulsating 3-2 defeat to defending champions Argentina. The 23-year-old cut in from the left wing and cur

item.title: Onitsha drug market hands over N435m fake, expired drugs to NAFDAC
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Onitsha drug market, popularly known as Ogbogwu Market at Bridge Head in Onitsha, Anambra State, on Monday handed over substandard and The post Onitsha drug market hands over N435m fake, expired drugs to NAFDAC appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Ipswich latest: Deal agreed for Brazil striker
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Editorial: The PFIPC controversy cannot be swept aside
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The bigger question many Nigerians are asking is simple: if the PFIPC does not exist, how did its name appear in the 2026 federal budget with over ₦1.3 billion allocated to it? Budget documents do not appear by magic. They pass through many government offices before they are approved by the National

item.title: Northern Christian leaders visit family of woman lynched in Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Responding from the hospital where he is receiving treatment, Aliyu thanked the Christian leaders for identifying with his family during their difficult time. The post Northern Christian leaders visit family of woman lynched in Kaduna appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: 2027: “Peter Obi Wants To Divide Nigeria” – Former Borno Governor Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Former Borno State Governor, Ali Modu Sheriff, has alleged that the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Peter Obi, wants to divide Nigeria. Sheriff made this allegation on Monday when he appeared as a guest in an interview on ‘Politics Today’, a programme on Channels Tele

item.title: Adeleke charges UNIOSUN’s new chancellor to sustain legacy of excellence
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Responding, Mrs Samson pledged to work with the university's management to mobilise resources for infrastructural development and strengthen support for both staff and students. The post Adeleke charges UNIOSUN’s new chancellor to sustain legacy of excellence appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: 2027: Northerners will never vote for Peter Obi – Ali Modu Sheriff
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Borno State Governor and senior All Progressives Congress (APC) figure, Ali Modu Sheriff, has ruled out the possibility of Peter Obi winning the presidency in 2027, insisting the former Anambra State governor cannot attract the backing of Northern voters. The post 2027: Northerners will never

item.title: 2027: ADC has no concrete rescue plan for Nigeria – Atiku’s ally, Aliyu dumps party, joins APC
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Aslam Aliyu, an ally of the African Democratic Congress, ADC, presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has dumped the party and joined the ruling All Progressives Congress, APC. In a statement, Aliyu said the ADC does not have a concrete rescue plan for Nigeria, stating that her decision followed a l

item.title: N152m Property row: Engineer takes Oak Homes to Appeal Court
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Although the High Court dismissed most of the developer's claims, it ruled that the conduct of both parties amounted to novation, thereby terminating the original agreement and limiting Ugbebor's remedy to a refund of the purchase price.

item.title: Northern Groups Condemn Alleged Exclusion Of North-West From N3.9trn Federal Road Projects
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Coalition of Northern Groups (CNG) has condemned the federal government over the alleged exclusion of the North-West geopolitical zone from the recently approved federal road projects valued at over ₦3.9 trillion. The coalition described the development as a betrayal of the region’s politi

item.title: If Belgium beat us, we’ll say it was rigged – Trump
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Donald Trump claims a US defeat to Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16 would be “rigged,” echoing his 2020 election claims. Read more on Trump’s comments.
Read More: https://punchng.com/if-belgium-beat-us-well-say-it-was-rigged-trump/

item.title: Tinubu moving Nigeria forward, wants country to progress – Ali Modu Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Borno State governor, Ali Modu Sheriff, on Monday, said President Bola Tinubu should be given kudos for moving Nigeria forward. Sheriff said Tinubu has done a lot and wants Nigeria to progress, adding that the president wants freedom for states. The former National Chairman of the All Progres

item.title: US judge tosses out Musk’s bid to void Twitter fraud verdict
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Jurors had found owner Elon Musk liable for tweets questioning whether Twitter was overrun by fake and spam accounts.

item.title: 2027: Atiku Is On His Own, It’s Not North’s Time Now — Ali Modu Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Sheriff expressed confidence that President Tinubu would secure re-election in 2027 but said the APC would not take anything for granted and would work hard ahead of the polls. The post 2027: Atiku Is On His Own, It’s Not North’s Time Now — Ali Modu Sheriff appeared first on Channels Tel

item.title: Kano Judicial Commission Retires Judge Over Bribery, Sanctions Court Officials
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Kano State Judicial Service Commission has orderd compulsory retirement of a Sharia Court judge over bribery and imposed disciplinary measures on several other judicial officers and court staff following investigations into allegations of misconduct.   The sanctions were approved during the

item.title: NPFL: Babaganaru laments Kano Pillars rejection
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Muhammad Babaganaru has expressed disappointment with his disenchantment by Kano Pillars. Babaganaru helped the Sai Masu maintained their top-flight status last season after a difficult start to the campaign. The experienced tactician side that he had already agreed on a contract extension with the

item.title: You are a despicable woman, Mbappe slams Paraguayan senator over racist remarks
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kylian Mbappe calls Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla a “despicable woman” over racist comments following France’s World Cup victory.

item.title: Crowds hold ‘destroy Israel’ banner at Spain’s Pamplona bull run
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A giant banner reading ‘Destroy Israel’ was unfurled as thousands gathered for the San Fermin bull run festival.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Cape Verde’s Cabral Beats Messi To Round Of 32 Best Goal Award
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Cape Verde defender Sidny Lopes Cabral has been voted the winner of the Goal of the Round of 32 award at the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026, Completesports.com reports. Cabral earned the honour for his stunning strike in Cape Verde’s thrilling 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentin

item.title: Micron’s stock gains, signaling a ‘return to optimism’ about the chip sector
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Investors are looking forward to Samsung’s earnings and SK Hynix’s ADR listing, experts say.

item.title: Best Buy and Apple flag a price shock for shoppers
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: India Awards ICCR Scholarships To 40 Nigerian Students, Seeks Stronger Bilateral Ties
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The High Commissioner of India to Nigeria, Ambassador Abhishek Singh, has urged 40 Nigerian students awarded the prestigious Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR) scholarships to become ambassadors of goodwill by strengthening the long-standing relationship between Nigeria and India through a

item.title: Neymar Bids Emotional Farewell To Brazil After World Cup Heartbreak
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Neymar has announced the end of his international career after Brazil’s shock elimination from the 2026 FIFA World Cup, bringing the curtain down on one of the most celebrated careers in the history of the Seleção. The 34-year-old forward made the emotional announcement following Brazil’s 2-1 defeat

item.title: How to Get the Most Out of Your Kid’s Trump Account
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Families with young kids can officially start putting money in a Trump Account as of July 4, 2026. But is this account right for you and your kid's savings goals and needs? Sarah Foster explains (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: FCMB Shareholders approve N23.08bn dividend after record profit
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: FCMB Group reported profit before tax of N202.1 billion for the year ended December 31, 2025, representing an 81 per cent increase from N111.9 billion recorded in the previous year. Profit after tax rose by 142 per cent to N177.3 billion, while gross revenue climbed 42.5 per cent to N1.13 trillion.

item.title: Love, deception and the real lives of Nigeria's romance fraudsters
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: In tonight's edition, France pulls all of its diplomats out of Burkina Faso in a tit-for-tat move after Ougadougou cut ties with Paris last month. Also, in Tanzania, young people are planning on taking to the streets for an anti-government protest on Tuesday. And Lagos's Yahoo Boys are behind a lucr

item.title: FG launches largest decentralised renewable energy projects in Adamawa, Kebbi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government on Monday, commenced the construction of 42 renewable energy projects in Adamawa and Kebbi states that will deliver nearly 30 megawatts of clean electricity to thousands of homes, businesses and public institutions, The post FG launches largest decentralised renewable energy

item.title: Death toll from Venezuela’s twin earthquakes rises to 3,535
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The government has not given a figure for those still missing under mountains of rubble, but the UN estimates that as many as 50,000 people may still be unaccounted for. The post Death toll from Venezuela’s twin earthquakes rises to 3,535 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Hamas Dissolves Gaza Governing Body To Pave Way For Civilian Administration
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Palestinian group Hamas has dissolved the body that has governed the Gaza Strip for nearly two decades, clearing the way for a technocratic committee to assume civilian administration of the war-ravaged and besieged enclave. The announcement on Monday marks one of the most significant political

item.title: Stocks Push Higher Led by Chipmakers | Closing Bell
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Emily Graffeo. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: TeraWulf’s stock gains after a $19 billion deal with Anthropic
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The deal “validates” the crypto-mining company’s pivot to supporting the AI buildout, its CEO said.

item.title: Bode market inferno: Oyo guber candidates donate cash, building materials to victims
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Governorship candidates of various political parties in Oyo State and other stakeholders have donated cash and building materials worth over The post Bode market inferno: Oyo guber candidates donate cash, building materials to victims appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Hoto’s PixelDrive screwdriver is down to $60, matching its best price
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: If your Prime Day purchases included a new desk, TV stand, bookshelf, or other furniture you still haven’t assembled, Hoto’s PixelDrive cordless screwdriver can help speed up the process. It’s currently on sale for $59.99 ($20 off) at Amazon, matching its best price to date.

item.title: Fery's fairy-tale Wimbledon run continues as Brit reaches quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Arthur Fery - Britain's last remaining hope in singles - kept his Wimbledon dream alive as he sealed a sensational five-set victory against Grigor Dimitrov to reach the quarter-finals.

item.title: KKR’s Private Credit Clients Slow Down Their Redemption Requests
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Investors in a KKR & Co. retail private credit fund got back all of their requested cash in the second quarter, a sign that individual investors’ skittishness over the asset class may be easing.

item.title: Death toll from Venezuela quakes rises to 3,535 as more than 17,000 left homeless
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The death toll from the back-to-back earthquakes that hit Venezuela on June 24 has risen to 3,535, official figures released on Monday showed. More than 17,000 people have been left homeless by the twin disasters.

item.title: Strategy is losing a lot of money on bitcoin. Here’s why it’s selling anyway.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Strategy sold more than 3,000 bitcoins to raise cash to pay dividends on its preferred stock — something Executive Chair Michael Saylor had previously said it didn’t need to do.

item.title: Adebutu, Wife Bag Chieftaincy Titles in Ogun
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) governorship candidate in Ogun State, Oladipupo Adebutu, and his wife, Yeye Adenike Adebutu, have been installed as the Babalaje and Yeye Babalaje of Iwoye-Ketu in Imeko-Afon Local Government Area of the state. The post Adebutu, Wife Bag Chieftaincy Titles in Ogun

item.title: Ekiti: Oyebanji visits family of dead kidnap victim
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: …beefs up security around border towns Ekiti State Governor, Biodun Oyebanji, has visited the family of 82-year-old Mrs. Rachael Olomi in Eda Oniyo Ekiti, who died while in the custody of kidnappers, assuring residents that his administration would intensify security operations around border communi

item.title: Police arrest woman for attempted murder of house help in Umuahia, recover knife
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Abia State Police Command says it has arrested a 35-year-old woman, Mrs Nwoko Gift, for attempting to harm her 12-year-old house help with a knife. According to the Abia State Police Command, the woman was seen in a viral video on social media attempting to kill the victim, identified as Miss Eb

item.title: Prosecutors in US lay out case against alleged killer of Charlie Kirk
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Judge to decide whether there's enough evidence to bring Tyler Robinson to trial for conservative activist's killing.

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader’s Funeral Procession Attracts Huge Crowd
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A mammoth crowd gathered in Tehran, the Iranian capital on Monday for the third day to mourn the slain Spiritual leader of the Islamic Republic Ali Khamenei as authorities and supporters called for revenge against the US and Israel. Mourners threw flower petals on the coffins of Ali Khamenei and his

item.title: FA considering appeal after Quansah red card
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: The Football Association is considering whether to appeal after England defender Jarell Quansah's red card against Mexico.

item.title: Dell’s stock gets another Trump bump — but this time it’s fading
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The president recommended that people buy Dell computers while speaking about the launch of “Trump accounts” on Monday.

item.title: Why I Remain Active After Years in Public Service, By Senator Obanikoro

item.description: Jonathan Eze Former Minister of State for Defence and politician, Senator Musiliu Obanikoro, on Monday, said his unwavering commitment to public service and the development of Lagos State remains the

item.title: Prince Harry Arrives In UK Amid Security Dispute
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: He is last understood to have met the king, who is being treated for an undisclosed form of cancer, at the monarch's London residence, Clarence House, in September 2025. The post Prince Harry Arrives In UK Amid Security Dispute appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Traders Are Most Positive on Dollar Since 2015 as Fed Hike Looms
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Global traders have turned the most positive on the outlook for the dollar since 2015 as bets that borrowing costs will remain elevated for longer have fueled a monthlong rally in the US currency.

item.title: 32 Killed in 24 hours as road carnage rocks Nigeria
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The accident involved a MAN truck and a Toyota Sienna commercial bus, leaving seven people dead-six male adults and one female adult, while three male adults sustained injuries. The injured were taken to Abobo General Hospital, with a preliminary investigation identifying tyre burst as the cause of

item.title: US Supreme Court clears path for Texas to enforce app age verification law
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Parents must approve app downloads for minors as legal fight over Texas age verification law continues.

item.title: OPEC+ raises output but cartel's future hangs in balance
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: A handful of OPEC+ countries have agreed to increase their output targets from August but the cartel is fighting for its survival after the UAE's departure. A French company is using the Seine to cool down Paris' buildings. And, Sky says it's buying ITV's broadcast channels and streaming service for

item.title: Don’t resort to sabotage, APC tells aggrieved aspirants, confirms candidate replacements
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The ruling All Progressives Congress APC has assured its aggrieved aspirants of a fair hearing, urging them to embrace internal dispute resolution mechanisms rather than actions capable of sabotaging the party. The post Don’t resort to sabotage, APC tells aggrieved aspirants, confirms candidat

item.title: Tether’s Former CIO Plans Sale of Stake in Stablecoin Giant
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Richard Heathcote, who until earlier this year oversaw stablecoin giant Tether Holdings SA’s investment portfolio, is planning to sell a small stake in the company, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Hedge Funds Turn Most Negative on the Japanese Yen Since 2007
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Hedge funds turned the most negative on the yen since 2007 as the currency trades near its weakest level in four decades.

item.title: Microsoft Cuts 4,800 Jobs as Xbox Restructuring Continues
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Francis Onyemachi reports more layoffs expected: Microsoft has laid 4,800 employees, with Xbox accounting for a large share. Amy Coleman, Microsoft executive vice president, revealed this in a memo to employees, noting that the tech giant needed to focus on areas that can deliver for customers amid

item.title: Nigerians starving to death under you – ADC to Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The African Democratic Congress, ADC, has described reports that more than 17 million Nigerians, including infants and young children, are facing acute hunger as a growing humanitarian disaster created by the President Bola Tinubu administration’s incompetence, misplaced priorities and failed

item.title: Body of Iran’s slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei arrives in Qom
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s body arrives in Qom as millions of mourners honour his leadership over past three days.

item.description: Bloomberg's Mark Gurman said that despite ups and downs for Apple and Broadcom's partnership to build out AI servers is expected to last for multiple generations of Apple products. Gurman said that relationship is mutually beneficial as Apple needed Broadcom to help build out the technology and Appl

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE) has announced it will, on July 23, 2026, host the 4th Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture in Lagos. The post NGE to host 4th Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture July 23 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Czech-Nigeria Partnership Opens New Frontier In 3D Printing Skills Development
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A partnership between the Czech Republic and Nigeria has opened a new frontier in additive manufacturing with the launch of a 3D printing training and capacity-building programme aimed at equipping young Nigerians with practical innovation skills. The programme, inaugurated in Abuja, is a collaborat

item.title: Over 60,000 ADC members defect to APC in Kebbi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: More than 60,000 members and supporters of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Jega Local Government Area of Kebbi State have defected to the All Progressives Congress (APC). The defectors were received by Kebbi State Governor, Nasir Idris, at the Government House in Birnin Kebbi on Monday, acc

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents

item.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

item.description: Maverick singer Habeeb Okikiola Badmus, popularly known as Portable, has defended First Lady, Remi Tinubu’s small scale business advice to unemployed Nigerian women. DAILY POST reports that Mrs Tinubu received backlash after urging unemployed Nigerian women to embark on frying bean cake popula

item.title: Northern Christian Leaders Demand Justice For Lynched Kaduna Woman
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Prominent Christian leaders from Northern Nigeria on Monday visited the family of the late Ummulkhairi Usman Aliyu, the Muslim woman lynched by a mob in Maraban Jos, Kaduna State, and called for justice over her killing. The delegation, led by the chairman of the Northern Christian Association (NCA)

item.title: EXCLUSIVE — World Cup 2026: Bonfrere Backs France For Final, Says Teamwork Drove Super Eagles’ Golden Generation
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Nigeria coach Johannes Bonfrere has predicted that the individual brilliance of Kylian Mbappe, Ousmane Dembele and other top stars will propel France to the final of the FIFA World Cup 2026, Completesports.com reports. Speaking on Sunday from his country home in the Netherlands, the Dutch tac

item.title: ONGA Ɗanɗano: Bringing the authentic taste of Arewa to Northern kitchens
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: For generations, Northern Nigeria cooking has been defined by a careful combination of ingredients, herbs and spices with each ingredient contributing its own unique flavour. The post ONGA Ɗanɗano: Bringing the authentic taste of Arewa to Northern kitchens appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Nigeria Lost N2.376tn To Grid Underutilisation In 12 Years – NISO
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Managing Director/Chief Executive Officer of the Nigerian Independent System Operator (NISO), Engr. Abdu Bello, has disclosed that Nigeria lost over N2.376tn to power sector grid underutilisation in capacity payments since 2015. Bello said this at the 11th Nigeria Energy Forum 2026 (NEF-2026) Co

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Brazil take decision on sacking Ancelotti after elimination
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Brazil have opted to stick with Carlo Ancelotti as their head coach, despite their early elimination from the 2026 World Cup. The Selecao suffered a shock 2-1 defeat to Norway in the last-16 of the tournament on Sunday. The result means that their wait for their sixth title continues. But Brazil&#82

item.title: Python 3.14 compiled to metal – no interpreter
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/can1357/pon Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48809496 Points: 29 # Comments: 9

item.title: Kano Police Impound 81 Vehicles Over Number Plate Violations
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Kano State Police Command has impounded 81 vehicles for operating with covered number plates, defaced registration numbers or no registration, warning that offenders will be prosecuted as part of efforts to curb crime and improve public safety. The Commissioner of Police in the state, Ibrahim Ba

item.title: AI actress Tilly Norwood to star in feature film
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The movie title "Misaligned" refers to the notion of the struggle to make AI align with human values and goals. The post AI actress Tilly Norwood to star in feature film appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Tinubu commissions Body of Benchers’ office annex in Abuja
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, GCFR, has commissioned the newly completed Office Annex for the Body of Benchers, located in the Institutional and Research District of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Abuja. Speaking during the commissioning ceremony in Abuja on Monday, Tinubu said the state-of-the

item.title: Osun: Police warn cultists against planned July activities
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Osun State Police Command has warned cult groups and their members against engaging in any activity to commemorate July 7, 2026. The warning is coming after intelligence reports of a planned cult-related celebrations across the country on Tuesday, July 7. In a statement issued by the Police Publ

item.title: NATO urges members to deliver plans for reaching defence spending targets
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US warns of consequences for NATO members without clear spending plans, signalling high expectations from allies.

item.title: World Cup 2026 : Watching Mexico-England in one Boston's Hispanic outskirts
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Mexico-England ended in a 3-2 victory for the Three Lions at the Azteca Stadium in Mexico City. In Chelsea, on the outskirts of Boston, the Mexican community turned out in force to watch the game.

item.title: Hertz Bears Boost Short Bets to Record After Shares Sink 60%
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Short sellers are anticipating more pain ahead for Hertz Global Holdings Inc. after the stock plummeted almost 60% in less than two weeks.

item.title: FG paid ₦358.3bn to cushion electricity tariffs in first quarter of 2026 — NERC
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government spent ₦358.32 billion on electricity subsidies between January and March 2026 to offset the gap between the actual cost of electricity generation and the tariffs paid by consumers, the Nigerian Electricity Regulatory Commission (NERC) has disclosed. The regulator said the subs

item.title: Court orders remand of 3 suspects over killing of ex-OGTV broadcaster, guard
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The order followed an ex parte application filed by the Ogun State Police Command seeking the suspects' remand while investigations and prosecutorial review are concluded. The post Court orders remand of 3 suspects over killing of ex-OGTV broadcaster, guard appeared first on Vanguard News.
